prompt: This code throws SQLException: ResultSet not positioned on a row. What is the bug?
choices: getString must be called with a column index (1), not a column name
rs.next() must be called to advance the cursor to the first row before reading any column values
executeQuery returns null when the row exists
PreparedStatement must use setInt to bind the id before executing
explanation: A freshly executed ResultSet cursor is positioned before the first row — no row is current. Calling getString() before rs.next() throws an exception. Fix: if (rs.next()) { System.out.println(rs.getString("name")); } — rs.next() advances the cursor and returns true if a row exists.
